Tomato_Early_Blight.cause: Fungal infection caused by Alternaria solani, often thriving in humid conditions.
Tomato_Early_Blight.pesticide: Apply Mancozeb or Chlorothalonil based fungicides every 7-10 days.
Tomato_Early_Blight.fertilizer: Use Potassium-rich NPK fertilizer to strengthen plant cell walls.
Tomato_Early_Blight.prevention: Ensure proper spacing for airflow, remove lower infected leaves, and avoid overhead watering.

Tomato_Late_Blight.cause: Oomycete pathogen Phytophthora infestans, highly destructive in cool, wet weather.
Tomato_Late_Blight.pesticide: Copper-based fungicides or systemic fungicides like Metalaxyl.
Tomato_Late_Blight.fertilizer: Balanced organic compost to improve overall plant vigor.
Tomato_Late_Blight.prevention: Plant resistant varieties, destroy infected debris, and monitor weather for high-risk periods.

Apple_Scab.cause: Fungus Venturia inaequalis that overwinters in fallen leaves.
Apple_Scab.pesticide: Captan or Myclobutanil fungicides during the green tip stage.
Apple_Scab.prevention: Rake and destroy fallen leaves in autumn to reduce inoculum levels.

Healthy.cause: N/A - Plant is in optimal condition.
Healthy.fertilizer: Standard maintenance fertilizer according to crop type.
Healthy.prevention: Continue good agricultural practices: proper watering, sunlight, and soil health.
